fieldData.post-body:
Spec homes are the homes that need to sell the fastest.

Yet they’re often the ones marketed the least strategically.

Across the homebuilding industry, spec homes are frequently treated as a simple listing exercise. Once construction wraps, a photoshoot is scheduled, the images are uploaded to the MLS, and the home is added to the builder’s website.

From there, teams wait for buyers to find it.

But in today’s market, that approach leaves a significant gap between a home being available and a home being actively marketed to sell quickly.

And that gap can be expensive.

‍

The hidden cost of slow spec home absorption

When a spec home sits on the market longer than expected, the impact extends far beyond the sales team.

Every additional day carries real costs:

• Interest on construction financing
• Carrying costs for utilities and maintenance
• Opportunity cost tied to capital sitting in inventory
• Sales momentum slowing within the community

For builders managing multiple specs at once, even a small delay in absorption can ripple across the entire pipeline.

Marketing teams often focus on generating traffic broadly, but spec homes require something more specific: focused visibility and emotional buyer connection for a single home.

‍

Why listing photos alone aren’t enough anymore

Traditional listing photography is essential. It establishes quality, shows the product clearly, and gives buyers an initial sense of the home.

But today’s buyers are discovering homes in very different ways than they did even five years ago.

Most first interactions happen through:

• Social media feeds
• Builder websites
• digital advertising
• mobile browsing late at night
• links shared between family members

In these environments, static listing photos often struggle to capture attention long enough to move a buyer from scrolling to serious interest.

What buyers respond to now is something closer to a story.

They want to understand how the home feels.
They want to imagine their life inside it.
They want to quickly grasp what makes this particular home different.

That kind of connection rarely happens with a simple gallery of images.

‍

Spec homes need structured storytelling

Instead of treating spec homes as static listings, forward-thinking builders are beginning to market them as short campaigns.

This approach focuses on telling a clear, structured story about the home across multiple pieces of content.

For example, one spec home can produce:

• Listing photography for MLS and website
• Social media video walkthroughs
• short-form reels highlighting key spaces
• twilight imagery that stops buyers mid-scroll
• marketing assets for email campaigns
• visual content for the sales team to share directly with prospects

Each piece plays a different role in helping buyers discover, understand, and emotionally connect with the home.

Rather than relying on a single moment of exposure, the home becomes visible across multiple touchpoints where buyers are already spending time.

‍

Marketing that supports sales velocity

For builders, the real goal of marketing isn’t simply engagement or impressions.

It’s sales velocity.

The faster a spec home sells, the more efficiently capital moves through the pipeline and the stronger the overall health of the community becomes.

Strategic visual content can help accelerate that process by:

• increasing online engagement with the listing
• generating stronger buyer curiosity before showings
• giving sales teams better tools to share with prospects
• creating momentum around a specific home

In other words, marketing moves from being a passive listing asset to an active part of the sales process.

‍

A shift builders are starting to make

More builder marketing teams are beginning to ask a different question:

Instead of asking: “Do we have photos for the listing?”

They’re asking: “How do we create a complete marketing story for this home?”

That shift changes how content is planned, produced, and used.

It turns a photoshoot into a strategic marketing asset rather than a single deliverable.

‍

The opportunity in front of builder marketing teams

Spec homes represent one of the clearest opportunities for builders to connect marketing directly to measurable results.

With the right approach, the same home that might quietly sit as a listing can instead become a focused campaign that draws attention, creates interest, and supports faster sales.

For marketing leaders looking to maximize their efforts, the question is no longer whether content matters.

The question is whether the content being created is structured to actually help the home sell.

‍

Want to see how builders are testing this approach?

DIG recently launched the FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge, designed to test whether structured visual storytelling can measurably reduce days on market for spec homes.

Five homes. One structured content strategy. A measurable result.

If days on market don’t drop by at least seven days, the builder doesn’t pay.

‍

‍
fieldData.name: The Spec Home Marketing Gap Most Builders Ignore

fieldData.post-body:
Four DIG Projects Earn National Recognition at IBS

Each year, the National Association of Home Builders honors the best in the industry during the International Builders’ Show. The Nationals celebrate excellence in new home sales and marketing across the country, recognizing the communities, experiences, and creative execution that truly move the industry forward.

This year, four DIG projects received national recognition. We are proud to partner with the builders and teams behind each of them.

‍

Best Sales Experience: SHAWOOD Folsom Sales Office & Inspirational Design Gallery

‍

Winner: Best Sales Experience

A sales experience should do more than present a product. It should tell a story, build confidence, and create connection.

The SHAWOOD Folsom Sales Office & Inspirational Design Gallery was designed to immerse buyers in craftsmanship, detail, and possibility. Every visual touchpoint supported the brand story. Every space reinforced quality.

At DIG, our role is to ensure that experience translates visually across platforms. From photography to digital assets, we focus on clarity, precision, and emotion so the experience resonates long before a buyer steps through the door.

‍

Best Website for Builder: SHAWOOD

Winner: Best Website for Builder

A builder’s website is often the first showing.

The SHAWOOD at Folsom website pairs thoughtful design with strong visual storytelling. Clean architecture. Refined interiors. A digital journey that mirrors the in person experience.

High performing websites do not rely on information alone. They rely on imagery that builds trust instantly. Our team captures and produces content with downstream use in mind, ensuring every image works as hard online as it does in print, social, and sales environments.

‍

Best Interior Merchandise: Toll Brothers, Heights at DTC – The Prentice Model

Winner: Best Interior Merchandise

Model homes should feel layered, intentional, and livable.

The Prentice Model by Toll Brothers at Heights at DTC demonstrates how design selections and merchandising elevate a space from staged to aspirational. Every finish, fixture, and furnishing works together to create a cohesive emotional experience.

Our job is to capture those details with precision. Lighting, composition, tonal balance. The small elements that make great design feel tangible on screen.

‍

Detached Community of the Year: SHAWOOD at Folsom

‍

Winner: Detached Community of the Year

Community of the Year is about more than architecture. It is about vision, execution, and buyer connection.

SHAWOOD at Folsom stands out for its commitment to craftsmanship and thoughtful design at every scale, from individual homes to the overall neighborhood experience.

When a community earns national recognition, it reflects a collective effort. Builder. Designers. Marketers. Sales teams. Creative partners. We are honored to play a role in helping that vision be seen clearly and consistently.

‍

Why This Matters

Recognition at the national level reinforces an important truth: great communities deserve great storytelling.

At DIG, we approach every project as a long term asset, not a one time shoot. We produce builder focused visuals designed to support websites, campaigns, sales centers, and ongoing marketing efforts.

To our builder partners, thank you for trusting us to help tell your story.

If your marketing team is ready for visuals that support award level communities and measurable results, we would love to connect.

‍
fieldData.name: Four DIG Projects Earn National Recognition at IBS

fieldData.post-body:
Stop Creating Content. Start Creating Assets.

Most homebuilders are not struggling because they lack content. They are struggling because the content they create only lives in one place, one time, for one purpose.

A photo shoot happens. Images get uploaded. A few are used on a listing. A handful make it to social. Then everything goes quiet until the next shoot.

That is not a content strategy. That is content disposal.

The builders who are moving homes faster are not creating more content. They are creating assets that work harder and last longer.

A single shoot should never have a single job.

‍

‍

When visuals are planned with intention, one shoot can fuel website hero imagery that sets the tone for the entire brand experience. Those same visuals should appear in email campaigns that keep prospects warm and engaged. They should be formatted and optimized for social ads that stop the scroll and drive action. They should live inside the sales center on screens that reinforce value and confidence during in person conversations. They should be ready to refresh listings so inventory never feels stale or overlooked.

This is how modern builder marketing works. A long term visual strategy that continues to deliver value shoot after shoot.

The mistake most teams make is treating photography as a box to check instead of infrastructure that supports every channel. When visuals are captured without downstream use in mind, marketing teams are forced to stretch assets beyond their limits or scramble to fill gaps later.

That scramble costs time. It slows momentum. It creates inconsistency. And it quietly works against sales.

‍

A Simple Way to Audit Your Current Visual Strategy

Before you plan your next shoot, ask these questions:

  • Where will these images live beyond the listing?
  • Which images are intended for website hero placement?
  • What visuals will support email campaigns or paid social?
  • How will these assets show up inside the sales center?
  • What content will refresh existing inventory?

If you cannot answer these questions before the shoot happens, the visuals are being created without a strategy. That is where most marketing teams get stuck.

Your headcount isn't going to increase this year, but your demands are. And that’s why we exist. Our business was created to help lean marketing teams do more, with less effort. DIG works with marketing teams to map visuals to specific channels so every shoot supports more than one goal and continues delivering value long after it wraps.

‍
fieldData.name: Stop Creating Content. Start Creating Assets.
Davies Imaging Group Launches FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge for Homebuilders
fieldData.post-body:
FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE

Davies Imaging Group Launches FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge for Homebuilders

Program measures changes in days on market across five vacant spec homes using a standardized visual deployment workflow

Roseville, CA | January 27, 2026 — Davies Imaging Group (DIG), a national provider of photography, videography, and virtual visual content for the homebuilding industry, today announced the launch of the FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge, a structured program designed to help builders evaluate whether standardized visual production and consistent online deployment can reduce days on market for vacant spec inventory without relying on price reductions.

The FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge is built around a defined implementation and measurement framework. Participating builders apply the same visual workflow across a fixed set of listings, deploy the content across specified buyer intent channels, and compare resulting days on market performance against a preestablished baseline.

“Many builders are not short on demand. They are managing the operational cost of time,” said Chad Davies, Founder of Davies Imaging Group. “This program is designed to measure whether consistent execution including photography, video, staging, and distribution can shorten time to sale under real listing conditions.”

‍

Program structure and measurement methodology

Under the FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge, participating builders:

• Establish a baseline for average days on market using homes closed within the previous 60 days, based on the builder’s primary listing source such as MLS days on market, Zillow Days on Zillow, or internal CRM, defined during onboarding.

• Apply FrameFlow across five vacant spec homes within active communities. For the purposes of the program, vacant refers to homes without physical staging at the time of content production.

• Deploy standardized visual assets, including virtual staging and motion based video, across designated channels such as builder listing websites and major consumer listing platforms.

• Measure outcomes over the agreed evaluation period while documenting material changes that may affect comparability, including price adjustments, incentive changes, contract fall throughs, or listing withdrawals.

The five home sample size is intended to balance statistical relevance with implementation consistency, allowing builders to observe directional performance without introducing excessive operational variability.

‍

Performance credit terms

Builders who achieve a seven day reduction in average days on market across the five participating homes, measured against the program’s defined baseline and subject to the stated inclusion and exclusion criteria, receive a credit of the program fee. This credit may be applied toward a long term FrameFlow subscription.

Full eligibility requirements, exclusions, and credit application terms are provided during qualification and onboarding.

‍

Availability

The FrameFlow Sell Faster Challenge is open to qualified homebuilders nationwide. Participation is limited to support consistent implementation and measurement across participating builders.

‍

About Davies Imaging Group

Davies Imaging Group is a national provider of photography, videography, and visual marketing solutions built specifically for homebuilders. DIG partners with builders to standardize visual production workflows, improve online presentation of vacant inventory, and support scalable content deployment across communities and markets.

‍

Media Contact
Chad Davies, CEO & Founder
chad@daviesimaging.com | 916-316-6787
Davies Imaging Group
Roseville, CA with nationwide operations

‍

How Top Builders Are Cutting Days on Market Without Discounting

Every builder tracks days on market, but most underestimate how expensive those days really are.

When a home sits:

  • Carry costs accumulate

  • Buyer urgency fades

  • Sales teams lose momentum

  • Marketing assets go stale

And yet, the default response is usually the same: price adjustments, incentives, or waiting it out.

But speed isn’t a pricing problem. It’s a presentation and execution problem.

‍

Why Most Homes Sit Longer Than They Should

‍

It’s rarely because buyers aren’t interested. More often, it’s because:

  • Listings don’t help buyers emotionally commit

  • Visuals don’t show how the home lives

  • Content is static in a dynamic buying environment

  • Homes look vacant, cold, or unfinished online

Today’s buyers don’t just browse, they decide online first. If the listing doesn’t create clarity and confidence immediately, they move on.

Speed favors the listings that do the heavy lifting upfront.

‍

Faster Sales Come From Better Visual Systems, Not More Marketing

‍

Selling faster isn’t about adding more content. It’s about deploying the right content, in the right sequence, in the right places.

High-performing listings share three traits:

  1. They visually tell a lifestyle story

  2. They reduce uncertainty for the buyer

  3. They create urgency without pressure

This is where motion-based visuals, strategic staging, and placement matter. When buyers can understand the home faster, they decide faster.

‍

Why Builders Don’t Test This Aggressively Enough

‍

Most builders suspect better visuals would help. But they don’t go all in because:

  • Testing feels risky

  • Results feel subjective

  • Implementation takes effort

  • There’s fear of no clear payoff

So they dabble.  And dabbling doesn’t move the needle. Real results require full execution and  not half measures.

‍

That’s why we created the FrameFlow Sell-Faster Challenge.

‍

Not a demo.
Not a theory.
A real-world performance test.

‍

Builders, here’s what you’ll do:

1. Onboarding & Baseline Establishment
 Provide your average days on market based on homes closed within the last 60 days.

2. FrameFlow Activation
 Virtually stage five (5) vacant homes, each with eight (8) or more enhanced images, and create a FrameFlow video for every listing.

3. Quality Control Review
 Participate in a Quality Control Review meeting to ensure buyer-ready execution and alignment prior to deployment.

4. Listing Deployment
 Publish all five (5) FrameFlow videos to the corresponding listing websites and Zillow listings.

5. Social Distribution
 Post five (5) FrameFlow videos across your social media channels, tagging Davies Imaging Group.

6. Transformation Review
 Participate in a post-deployment review to assess performance and share results publicly.

‍

As a reward for trying a new process with real capital - when your “FrameFlow Challenge” homes sell 7 days faster, your investment is refunded.

Yes, this is counterintuitive. Yes this is real. Your fantastic results will be rewarded with a clear mind on how to move forward.

Because our goal isn’t to sell five homes faster — it’s to help you build a system that sells every home faster.

‍

Cutting days on market doesn’t just improve one listing. It:

  • Improves cash flow
  • Reduces operational drag
  • Strengthens sales confidence
  • Creates a repeatable advantage

‍

In 2026, builders who win won’t just build better homes. They’ll move them faster with intention.

If you’re ready to stop guessing and start measuring, the FrameFlow Challenge is designed to prove its value by paying you back.

‍

‍Apply for the FrameFlow Sell-Faster Challenge below and mention “FrameFlow Challenge.”

‍
fieldData.name: How Top Builders Are Cutting Days on Market Without Discounting

fieldData.post-body:
Why Top Homebuilders Are Rethinking Their Photography Partners in 2026

For years, photography was treated as a check-the-box task in homebuilding. Capture the model. Photograph the spec. Upload to the MLS. Move on.

That mindset is shifting, and fast.

As buyer behavior evolves and competition intensifies, top homebuilders are rethinking what they actually need from their photography partners in 2026. The conversation is no longer about who can take the prettiest photos. It’s about who understands the full sales ecosystem and can help content work harder at every stage of the buyer journey.

‍

Here’s why the old approach is being challenged, and what builders are looking for now.

‍

1. Buyers Decide Long Before They Walk a Model

Today’s buyers form opinions early. Often before they ever schedule a tour. Often before they ever speak to a sales counselor.

Photography now plays a critical role in:

  • First impressions on social and paid ads

  • Emotional buy-in on websites and landing pages

  • Confidence during the research phase

Top builders recognize that photography is no longer just documentation, it’s persuasion. The right visual story builds trust, sets expectations, and influences whether a buyer takes the next step.

‍

2. One-Size-Fits-All Content Doesn’t Work Anymore

‍

Builders aren’t selling one product. They’re selling:

  • Lifestyle

  • Community

  • Quality

  • Differentiation

That means photography needs to be intentional. Strategic. Tailored.

Leading builders are moving away from partners who simply “show up and shoot” and toward teams who ask smarter questions:

  • Who is this community for?

  • What objections do buyers typically have?

  • What needs to be felt before it’s understood?

Photography partners who understand positioning, not just angles, are becoming essential.

‍

3. Content Is Now a Sales Tool, Not Just a Marketing Asset

‍

In 2026, photography lives everywhere:

  • Sales presentations

  • CRM follow-ups

  • Social media

  • Email campaigns

  • Print, signage, and digital displays

Top builders want photography partners who understand how content is actually used downstream, not just how it looks on delivery day.

The shift is clear: visuals must support sales goals, not just marketing aesthetics.

‍

4. Speed, Consistency, and Scalability Matter More Than Ever

With multiple communities, rapid releases, and ongoing campaigns, builders need partners who can scale with them without sacrificing quality or brand consistency.

‍

This means:

  • Reliable turnaround times

  • Consistent visual standards across markets

  • Teams who understand builder workflows

Builders are rethinking partnerships that create friction instead of momentum.

‍

5. The Best Partners Think Like Builders

The most valued photography partners in 2026 don’t just understand cameras. They understand construction timelines, sales cycles, model merchandising, and buyer psychology.

They know:

  • What details matter to buyers

  • How to highlight value without overselling

  • When to push creative, and when clarity wins

This builder-first mindset is what separates vendors from true partners.

‍

The Bottom Line

Top homebuilders aren’t abandoning photography. They’re elevating it.

In 2026, the most successful builders are partnering with teams who understand that photography is not about pretty pictures… Photography is about performance, storytelling, and influence. 

Because when your visuals are strategic, aligned, and purposeful, they don’t just showcase homes - they help sell them.

‍
fieldData.name: Why Top Homebuilders Are Rethinking Their Photography Partners in 2026

fieldData.post-body:
Why Photography for Builders Is Different

Buyers make decisions fast — and often online.

When visuals are clean, balanced, and intentional, they send a message:

  • This builder pays attention
  • This home is well-built
  • What you see is what you’ll get

That’s the silent power of precision in homebuilder marketing.

‍

The Psychology of “Feels Right”

Great visuals create emotional confidence.

If something feels slightly off — shadows, crooked lines, wrinkled rugs — buyers instantly notice (even subconsciously).

Precision = Trust
Trust = Sales Velocity

Even if buyers aren’t pointing out the exact issue, their brain already has.

‍

Precision in Action: The DIG Standard

These are the invisible steps we take that turn a photo from “good” to scroll-stopping:

  • Straightening blinds until every line matches
  • Hiding cords, clutter, tags, vents
  • Balancing light across every room
  • Choosing angles that sell flow and function
  • Perfecting composition through micro-adjustments

You don’t notice these things… and that’s the point.

‍

Brand Consistency Across Every Market

When builders expand into multiple communities and states, one thing often breaks: visual consistency.

Different photographers = different:

  • Styles
  • Standards
  • Editing
  • Brand perception

DIG solves that.
We ensure your brand looks like your brand — anywhere you build.

‍

How FrameFlow Streamlines the Whole Process

Builders move fast. Content delivery should too.

With FrameFlow, you get:

  • Rapid turnarounds
  • Centralized delivery
  • Approval workflows
  • Marketing-ready files — instantly

Speed meets scalability — without compromising the shot.

‍

Why This All Matters

Because the details do sell the home.
Clean visuals say: We care about the craft.
Polished storytelling says: We care about your trust.
Consistency says: We deliver quality everywhere.

And when someone says, “It slaps”?
We take that seriously.

‍
(Thank you for the finest ingredients.)

‍

Let’s Raise the Bar

If your visuals could work harder for your homes and your brand:

👉 See the precision in action → HERE
👉 Learn more about FrameFlow → HERE

Because when your homes look their best — your brand does too.

‍

fieldData.post-body:
Capture Architecture Like a Pro‍

Easy Smartphone Photography Tips

As a photographer with over 15 years of experience, I’ve captured thousands of buildings, and I’ve learned that you don’t need a fancy camera to take great shots. In fact, modern smartphones are powerful tools for photography! Below are some simple tips to help you take fantastic photos of buildings using just your phone.

Whether you’re capturing a cityscape or a cozy house, these techniques will make a big difference. Let's dive in!

‍

Tip 1: Camera Placement

Where you hold your phone can completely change the look of your photo. With just a few adjustments, you can make your building shots stand out.

  • Height Matters: Try holding your phone at different heights. For example, if you take a photo at eye level, you’ll capture the building as most people see it. But if you hold your phone above your head or closer to the ground, it can give a dramatic new perspective. Experiment with this by squatting down or raising your phone higher!
  • Move Closer or Farther: Getting too close to a building might make it look distorted (the lines won’t be straight). If you want the building to look more realistic, stand a bit farther back and zoom in. You’ll get a cleaner shot, and the building will look more natural.
  • Play with Zoom: Use your phone’s zoom feature to change the look of your photo. Wide-angle shots (like the “0.5x” setting on many phones) can make a building look huge and artistic. Zooming in (2x or more) gives a tighter, more accurate image. Try both to see what you like!
Tip 2: Framing Your Shot

Framing means how you place the building in your photo. It’s an easy way to make your shots look more professional.

  • Use Objects in the Foreground: Look around for things that can “frame” the building, like tree branches, people walking by, or other nearby structures. This helps guide the viewer’s eye to the building. For example, try taking a photo of a tall building with some tree leaves in the corner of the shot—it’ll make the photo more interesting!
  • Center or Off-Center? A simple trick is to place the building in the middle of the photo for a balanced look. Or, place the building off to one side to create a more dynamic composition. Both styles can look great, so try each one and see what you prefer.
  • Use the Background: If you're in a city, try using other buildings as a backdrop. Or if you’re in nature, mountains or clouds can add a stunning background. Experiment with what’s around you to create a more compelling shot.

‍

Tip 3: Find Unique Perspectives

Look at buildings from angles most people wouldn’t think of—this can turn an average photo into an amazing one!

  • Get Close: Stand closer to the building and look for cool details. Is there an interesting pattern on the wall or a reflection in the windows? Capture those up-close details to show the building in a new way.
  • Use Reflections: Rain puddles, glass windows, or even car mirrors can create stunning reflection shots of buildings. Reflections add an artistic touch, and it’s fun to experiment with how they change the look of your subject.
  • Shoot From Different Angles: Don’t be afraid to get creative with how you hold your phone. Lie down on the ground and shoot upward for a dramatic shot. Or try taking a photo from a higher place, like standing on a bench or balcony, to capture the building from above.

‍

Tip 4: Edit Your Photos

Editing can take a good photo and make it great. Luckily, your phone has built-in tools that make it easy to adjust your images.

  • Use Your Phone’s Built-In Tools: Start by using the editing features already in your photos app. Brightness, shadows, contrast, and vibrance are simple sliders that can improve your photo in seconds.
  • Straighten Your Lines: In building photography, crooked lines can make your photo look off. Use the "straighten" tool to make sure the building looks upright and parallel to the edges of your photo. This is especially helpful for capturing tall buildings.
  • Play With Color: Adjust the color balance to create a specific mood—make the colors warmer (more yellow) for a sunny, golden look, or cooler (more blue) for a clean, modern vibe.

‍

Tip 5: Prepare Your Phone Camera

Before you start shooting, make sure your phone is ready for action!

  • Turn on Grid Lines: This will help you line up the building perfectly and balance your shot. Most phones have this option in the camera settings.
  • Use 4:3 Aspect Ratio: Switch from the default “widescreen” (16:9) to “4:3” aspect ratio in your camera settings. This will give you more room to crop the image without losing quality.
  • Clean Your Lens: Always make sure your camera lens is clean. A quick wipe can make a huge difference in sharpness!

‍

Conclusion

With just a few easy adjustments and some creativity, you can capture stunning photos of buildings using only your phone. All images referenced on this page were captured and edited on my iPhone. Don’t be afraid to experiment with different angles, distances, and editing techniques. Remember, photography is all about practice—so get out there and start shooting!
‍

Chad Davies, CEO & Founder of Davies Imaging Group
fieldData.name: DYC Exclusive: Capture Architecture Like a Pro

Jason Crider has joined DIG as a Photography Manager. He is a familiar face to many of our clients, and his energy in the field is unmatched. After working on select projects alongside Chad Davies for the last five years, he has elected to transform our photography process by expanding our network and solidifying our training process. We are thrilled to have him join us as a full-time team member.

‍

‍Dani Martinelli has been promoted to Account Director. After a seven-year tenure with DIG, it’s clear that Dani’s focus on client experience is unmatched. With her commitment to service, she leads all client-facing interactions and will develop a team to support our growth.

‍

Thomas Brigantino has been promoted to Production Director. Since starting with DIG almost 18 months ago, Thomas has taken the reigns of our post-production process and helped assemble an incredible team of artists, technology, and processes. With his success in Post-Production, we’re excited to see how he impacts the entire scope of production in the coming years. 

‍

Makenna Lloyd has been promoted to Account Manager. Makenna joined DIG last summer with a Master's Degree from the University of Oregon. Her creativity and inquisitiveness have helped us manage hundreds of projects since her arrival. She has developed a new system for creating Photography Briefs and Debriefs while managing over a dozen vendor relationships. As we continue our growth, she will focus on client experience, streamlining art direction, and creating systems to retrieve in-depth project feedback.
fieldData.name: DIG Promotion Announcement

fieldData.post-body:
Introducing the DIG Alpha photography system, designed for the world’s best builders. ‍

Why are we releasing this content? Because homebuilding is a noble profession, and I believe creating new communities is a responsibility we shouldn’t take lightly. Regardless of your ability to work with my team at Davies Imaging Group, you should have access to the same tools we rely on to make new communities look their best. Our industry relies on your success.

– Chad Davies, CEO of Davies Imaging Group

This series of educational content is now available at www.daviesimaging.com/alpha
fieldData.name: DIG Alpha Announcement | Photography Success System for Builders

fieldData.post-body:
Last year Garman Homes kicked off 2023 by surprising Ava Bradley with the news of her being selected as a Hero Home recipient for the service and sacrifice of her late husband, US Army SSG Juantrea Bradley, Sr.  We kicked off 2024 by dedicating that home to Ava and her family at the Key Ceremony just outside Raleigh, in Fuquay-Varina.

Davies Imaging Group provided photography documentation services of the event on behalf of Garman Homes. This is the 3rd Hero Home built by Garman Homes, and the 27th Hero Home provided by Operation Coming Home, led by the HBA of Raleigh-Wake County.

See the full gallery here.
fieldData.name: Hero Home Key Ceremony

fieldData.post-body:
We have doubled down on our commitment to guarantee the highest levels of quality and the fastest turnaround times in our industry. Late last year, DIG started investment in a Mexico-based post-production operation. With early indications of success, we’re thrilled to introduce the following members of our team:

‍Maxine Mendoza has joined as a Post-Production Project Lead. 

Alejandro Aburto has joined as a Post-Production Team Lead.

Monica Muro has joined as a Post-Production Specialist.

Adriana Gutiérrez has joined as Post-Production Associate.

‍

DIG's purpose is to provide undeniable value to the world's best real estate companies. This expansion helps ensure that purpose is realized.

For more information about Davies Imaging Group, contact info@daviesimaging.com, or visit www.daviesimaging.com
fieldData.name: DIG Post-Production Team Announcement

fieldData.post-body:
Pro Builder's Forty Under 40 class of 2023 recognizes the brightest young talent and next-generation leaders and innovators in the home building industry. 

With a wide range of achievements, this year’s rising young stars are pushing the boundaries of personal and professional accomplishment, tapping a range of skills with a good dose of grit, ingenuity, adaptability, and a keen understanding of business and the ability to inspire and lead companies and teams.We’re excited to see Chad Davies, CEO of Davies Imaging Group, join this year’s class of energetic, innovative young leaders representing the future of the housing industry.
fieldData.name: ProBuilder Forty Under 40 Announcement

fieldData.post-body:
We’re committed to partnering with leaders in the residential new construction industry, like Do You Convert. This is the third year we’ve sponsored the DYC Summit, providing event coverage and portrait photography services throughout the two-day conference. 

‍

Thank you, DYC for bringing the brightest minds in our industry together for two days of connection, education, and inspiration. We appreciate everything you do!
fieldData.name: 2023 DYC Summit

fieldData.post-body:
We’re excited to announce that Makenna Lloyd has joined DIG as a Production Coordinator. Her journalism background and rich education experiences will make her an incredible addition to our team. Makenna impressed us with her intellectual depth, hysterical sense of humor, and her ambitions to become a Creative Director.

‍

We look forward to watching Makenna thrive at Davies Imaging Group.
fieldData.name: DIG Welcomes Makenna Lloyd

fieldData.post-body:
In our continual efforts to help elevate the industry, we helped cover this year’s Housing Innovation Summit, hosted at the University of Colorado in Denver. We contributed event documentation of multiple neighborhood tours, developer demonstrations, and a PropTech pitch fest. 

The America at Home study presented its two concept homes at the closing ceremonies of the conference. Earlier this year, Davies Imaging Group was asked to help document a design charet in San Diego, where the designs for these homes were originally conceived. The Summit represented a significant step in building more thoughtful, factory-constructed homes for a 21st-century buyer. Learn more about the America at Home Concept Homes:Picket Fence‍412 Concept
fieldData.name: 2023 Housing Innovation Summit

fieldData.post-body:
After six weeks of air time, Season 4 of HGTV’s Rock the Block Series had its season finale today. Our team had the privilege of photographing each of the homes after filming was wrapped back in November. After many months of waiting, we can finally share our work with the world!

‍

See the full gallery here: www.daviesimaging.com/rocktheblock
fieldData.name: Rock the Block Season 4

fieldData.post-body:
We’re excited to announce that Thomas Brigantino has joined our team in a full-time capacity to help facilitate growth on our post-production team.

Thomas brings a storied career managing global hospitality photography production for some of the world’s most recognizable brands. We’re excited to have him join us as we grow into new markets.
fieldData.name: DIG Welcomes Thomas Brigantino
2023 Nationals and BALA Awards
fieldData.post-body:
Congratulations to our clients for their success at The Nationals and BALA this week in Las Vegas. Three projects brought home gold:

  • Vineyard Views won Best Interior of a Custom Home, designed by Kimberly Timmons Interiors and Woodley Architectural Group.
  • Clubhouse at Lake Las Vegas by Del Webb won the BALA category for Best Community Amenity, designed by Kimberly Timmons Interiors.
  • Veterans Park Memorial won the BALA category for Best Affordable Housing Project by Danielian Architects.
